data.title: Sprint Data
data.csv_hint: CSV format: Sprint Name, Planned, Completed, Carried Over

learn.title: About Sprint Metrics
learn.velocity_body: Velocity measures how much work (in story points) a team completes per sprint. It's used for forecasting, not for performance evaluation. A team's velocity should be stable over time; sudden drops or spikes indicate issues worth investigating.
learn.burnup_body: A burn-up chart shows cumulative work done vs. total scope. It makes scope changes visible. A burn-down shows remaining work. Burn-up is preferred when scope changes frequently; burn-down is simpler when scope is fixed.
learn.forecast_body: Use average velocity to estimate how many sprints remain to reach the target scope. This gives a range, not a point estimate. The cone of uncertainty: forecasts get more reliable as you complete more sprints.
learn.p1: Velocity is not a performance metric — don't compare teams.
learn.p2: Inflating story point estimates to hit velocity targets defeats the purpose.
learn.p3: A single sprint's data is meaningless — use 3–5 sprint rolling averages.
learn.p4: Carried-over work is a signal, not just a number — investigate root causes.
